courses.title: Hazmat Awareness — Recertification
courses.summary: Recognize, isolate, and notify. Refresh your decision frame for hazardous-materials incidents.
courses.credibility: Built with NFPA 472 alignment and reviewed by 3 fire-service SMEs.

blocks.body: On arrival, your first 60 seconds are about *recognition*, not action. The placard, container shape, and bystander reports together build the picture.

blocks.prompt: You see a diamond placard with the number 1203 on a tanker. What does that suggest?

options.label: Gasoline (flammable liquid)
options.feedback: Correct — UN 1203 is gasoline. Your isolation distance defaults to 50m and grows with fire involvement.

options.label: Corrosive liquid
options.feedback: Not quite — corrosives use class 8 placards, not UN 1203.

options.label: Compressed oxygen
options.feedback: Oxygen is UN 1072 with a class 2.2 (oxidizer) placard.

blocks.body: Initial isolation is a *living* distance. It expands the moment you see vapor, heat, or victim symptoms — and contracts only after monitoring confirms the air.
A precise sitrep beats a long one. Lead with location, product, isolation, and resources requested.

courses.title: Sepsis Bundle — May Update
courses.summary: Recognize early sepsis, initiate the bundle within the hour, and document defensibly.
courses.credibility: Aligned to SSC 2024 guidance; reviewed by ED and ICU leads.

blocks.body: qSOFA isn't a diagnosis — it's a flag. Two of three (RR≥22, altered mentation, SBP≤100) raises the bar for re-eval, not for protocol auto-launch.

blocks.prompt: Which combination most strongly suggests qSOFA-positive?

options.label: RR 24, GCS 14, SBP 96
options.feedback: Right — that's three of three. Re-evaluate now and document the trigger.

options.label: HR 110, Temp 38.4, WBC 13
options.feedback: Those are SIRS-leaning vitals. qSOFA specifically uses RR, mentation, and SBP.

options.label: Lactate 2.1 alone
options.feedback: Lactate informs severity but isn't part of qSOFA.

blocks.body: Lactate, cultures, broad-spectrum, fluids, vasopressors if MAP <65 after fluids. Order matters less than *velocity*.
